reasoning: Marseille (Marignane) has a strong seasonal temperature cycle. The daily average in late October is typically around 15°C, dropping to ~10°C by late November and ~7°C by late January. The further into winter we go, the less likely a daily average will exceed the October 26 baseline. Using a standard deviation of ~3.5°C for daily anomalies, the difference distributions yield probabilities roughly around 36% for Nov 2, 16% for Nov 25, and <5% for Jan 24. I conservatively bump up the distant probabilities slightly to account for climate variance and the instruction to increase uncertainty.
